D9 Biology L3 Apply Reference

Autumn Colour — Chlorophyll Withdraws, Anthocyanins Bloom

in autumn the leaf reabsorbs chlorophyll to recover N; carotenoids that were there all summer suddenly become visible; and new anthocyanins are *actively* synthesised to colour the leaf red

The standard story (chlorophyll fades and the underlying yellow shows through) is half right. Yellow comes from carotenoids that were already in the leaf. But red comes from anthocyanins synthesised fresh in autumn — the tree spends energy to make them. Archetti (2009) argues this is a signal to leaf-mining aphids: a healthy red leaf advertises a tree well-defended.

Drag day of autumn. Chlorophyll decays; carotenoid stays; anthocyanin grows. The leaf swatch transitions green → yellow → red.
